summary: DHH expresses frustration with the lack of quality text editors available on Mac OS X when he switched to the platform. Coming from Windows where UltraEdit and TextPad were strong options, he found the Mac ecosystem lacking. The post announces TextMate as the solution to this gap, positioning it as the editor Mac users had been waiting for.
key_points: The Mac platform lacked a quality programmer's text editor comparable to Windows options like UltraEdit
DHH switched to Mac OS X around the Jaguar era (2002) and found the editor landscape disappointing
TextMate emerged as the answer to the 'missing editor' problem on OS X
money_quotes: When I first arrived on the Mac with Jaguar two years ago, I was somewhat stumped by the lack of a decent editor.
On Windows, I had been a big fan of UltraEdit and knew of TextPad a decent alternative. But on the Mac there was pretty much nothing of the kind.
key_insight: The Mac platform in the early 2000s had a glaring gap in programmer-friendly text editors that TextMate would go on to fill, becoming iconic in the Ruby on Rails community.
